<!--
STANDARDIZED CHAPTER TEMPLATE — apparatus standardized, narrative free.
Copy this file to NN-slug.qmd. Keep the section ORDER and the recurring callouts;
write the "period on its own terms" section freely (length flexes per period).
Weave the four questions into prose — do NOT use four rigid labelled subsections.
Every number must trace to a source; present disputed figures as teaching points.
Sources: story spine (narrative) · brief (analysis) · ledger (verdict) · compendium
(data) · datasets_by_topic (exercise) · module_reading_long_list (further reading).

THREE STANDING CONVENTIONS (every chapter):
1. PER-PARAGRAPH SOURCING. End each substantive paragraph with a footnote giving its
   sources + a "Read more:" pointer, e.g.  ...claim.^[**Sources:** Author (year).
   **Read more:** Author (year).]  Callouts carry an italic "Sources:" line instead.
   Keep prose clean (house style: provenance in the apparatus, not mid-sentence).
2. TWO RESEARCH SECTIONS, paired: "CLASSIC RESEARCH: the foundations" (early seminal
   economic-history work on the topic — the paradigms the chapter's debate descends from;
   JSTOR/archive links) THEN "AT THE RESEARCH FRONTIER" (recent cliometric work; sweep
   NBER/CEPR/RePEc + leading field venues, recent-first, one-line review + link). ALWAYS
   end with the verification-gate callout. NEVER fabricate a citation — verify every entry
   (Crossref/RePEc/JSTOR) before listing.
3. QUESTIONS — 3–5 essay/exam-style "Questions for consideration", PLUS a callout
   pointing to the book-level cross-cutting questions this chapter feeds (bank lives in
   the Conclusion).
-->

# Chapter title — a message-carrying sentence {#sec-chNN}

> *One-line chapter epigraph: the period in a sentence.*

## Follow one thing {.unnumbered}

<!-- The opening hook: one object that carries the whole period's story. -->

## Where we are on the arc {.unnumbered}

<!-- One paragraph locating the period on the spine + the recurring CoG locator map. -->

![Centre-of-gravity locator: where the weight sits this period.](figures/cog-locator-NN.png){#fig-cogNN}

## The stage and the cast

<!-- Geography/deep structures for the period. -->

::: {.callout-tip}
## Dramatis personae
The economic actors this period — and *what changed since last chapter*. **India appears
every chapter.**
:::

::: {.callout-note}
## How we know
The sources for this period, their gaps and biases, and the state of measurement.
:::

## The period on its own terms

<!-- The chronological, phased narrative (the "meat"). Flexes per period. From the story spine. -->

## Reading the period: the four questions

<!-- Woven prose, NOT four rigid headings. Direction · Channels · Modes · Europe.
     Use the "Follow the money" callout for the bullion/silver/capital tracer. -->

::: {.callout-important}
## Follow the money
The bullion/silver/capital tracer for this period — which way it ran, and what that tells
us about where the centre of gravity sat.
:::

## The verdict: where was the centre?

<!-- CoG verdict + an explicit confidence level (the evidence-ledger entry). -->

::: {.callout-warning}
## The debate
The live scholarly controversy of this chapter, presented as genuinely contested, with the
contending positions named.
:::

::: {.column-page}
**Data exhibit — [title].** The chapter's empirical centrepiece: one series/figure with
full provenance *and* caveats. *What you could do with this:* the research hook.
:::

## Threads forward

<!-- How the chapter advances the cross-cutting threads + the hand-off to the next chapter.
     End each paragraph with its ^[**Sources:** … **Read more:** …] footnote. -->

---

## Classic research: the foundations {.unnumbered}

<!-- Early seminal economic-history work on the topic — the paradigms/debates the chapter
     descends from. Each: citation · one-line review · JSTOR/archive link. VERIFY every entry. -->

- **Author (year)**, *Title* — what it established / which paradigm. [JSTOR/archive]

## At the research frontier: recent cliometric work {.unnumbered}

<!-- Recent-first; sweep NBER/CEPR/RePEc + leading field venues. Each: citation · one-line
     review tying it to a thread/debate · link (DOI/working-paper URL). VERIFY — never fabricate.
     Note honestly where the discipline's action actually is for this period. -->

- **Author et al. (year)**, "Title," *Venue* — one-line review + why it matters here. [DOI/URL]

::: {.callout-note}
## Keeping this current (verification gate)
Sweep date + repositories searched; every entry citation-verified. Refresh before publication;
**never list an unverified working paper**. Note where the discipline's action actually sits.
:::

---

### Questions for consideration {.unnumbered}

*Essay / exam style — reward the four-questions toolkit and the live debate, not recall.*

1.
2.
3.
4. <!-- 3–5 questions -->

::: {.callout-tip}
## Cross-cutting questions (collected at the end of the book)
The book closes with a bank of questions spanning several chapters. Those this chapter feeds:
- …
:::

### Data exercise {.unnumbered}

```{r}
#| label: ch-NN-exercise
#| eval: false
# Reproducible exercise on this chapter's dataset (datasets_by_topic.md).
```

### Key data {.unnumbered}

| Figure | Value | Source |
|---|---|---|
|  |  |  |

### Further reading {.unnumbered}

- **Core:**
- **Supplementary:**
- **The debate:**
